specialty: AR (mechanical) primary, some CR; auto repair shop niche website builder
min_ad_spend_usd: None — Google Ads not a core service; PPC available as separate add-on
contract_length_months: Month-to-month reported, with annual prepay discounts [uncertain]
cancellation_policy: Standard ~30-day notice; site goes dark on cancellation (vendor retains template + hosting) [uncertain]
asset_ownership: Vendor retains website template, hosting, and codebase. Customer typically owns their domain name (registered separately) but loses the site itself on cancellation. Content/copy ownership unclear. [uncertain]
pricing_transparency: Quote-only — 3 published plans referenced on site but exact dollar amounts gated behind a 10-minute call
known_pain_points: Generic template-driven sites with limited customization
Light SEO — local pages and meta tags only, no real content strategy or link-building
No native Google Ads management — must layer a separate vendor
No conversion tracking setup beyond basic form/phone analytics [uncertain]
Site disappears on cancellation; client retains nothing beyond domain
Pricing not transparent — must call to get quote
notable_clients: Thousands of small independent AR shops nationwide; no marquee names published
pros: Auto repair vertical specialist — 20+ years in the niche
Low entry monthly fee vs Hibu or full-service agencies
Bundled hosting + content updates + review widget
US-based support targeting AR shop owners specifically
cons: Template-based — sites look similar across customers, weak differentiation
Limited SEO depth — no link-building, no content marketing program
No paid media management (Google Ads, Meta Ads not core offering)
Customer does not own the site post-cancellation
Quote-only pricing creates friction and obscures comparison
Lacks CRM, call tracking, and shop management integrations (vs AutoLeap/Tekmetric)
koffman_relevance: Direct downgrade from Hibu on services breadth (no ads, no display, no listings management) but upgrade on transparency and contract friction. Would replace ONLY the website + basic SEO portion of Hibu, not the Google Ads or display layer. PSG would still need to layer paid media, conversion tracking, and CRM. Likely not a Hibu replacement; more of a cheaper bare-bones alternative if Koffman wanted to strip Hibu down to just hosting + template. Vendor lock-in (no site ownership) is similar to Hibu's pain point.
